questions.question: An alloy is referred to as amalgam, if it consists of which among the following metals?
questions.explanation: If an alloy consists of mercury with other metals, it will be referred to as amalgam.

questions.question: Atomic masses of elements are calculated is reference to isotope of which among the following elements?
questions.explanation: Carbon-12 isotope was chosen as the standard reference for measuring atomic masses. One atomic mass unit is a mass unit equal to exactly onetwelfth (1/12th) the mass of one atom of carbon-12.

questions.question: As per Law of Definite Proportions, elements are always present in a definite proportion by _____?
questions.explanation: In a pure chemical compound, elements are always present in a definite proportion by mass. This is known as the Law of Definite Proportions.

questions.question: Atoms of different elements with different atomic numbers, which have the same mass number, are known as _________?
questions.explanation: Atoms of different elements with different atomic numbers, which have the same mass number, are known as isobars.

questions.question: Van der Waals Forces doesn't include which among the following forces?
questions.explanation: Attractive intermolecular forces are known as van der Waals forces. van der Waals forces vary considerably in magnitude and include dispersion forces or London forces, dipole-dipole forces, and dipole-induced dipole forces.

questions.question: Which among the following chemical is added to LPG to detect leakages?
questions.explanation: LPG gas is basically propane and butane, and it is odorless in its natural state. The smell that you notice when there is a leak is the stench of an entirely different agent, called Ethyl Mercaptan. It possesses that strong odor of rotten cabbages. The smell helps us detect when there is a leak, which has safety and security perspective.

questions.question: Which among the following type of coal has highest calorific value?
questions.explanation: Anthracite has the highest carbon content, the fewest impurities, and the highest energy density of all types of coal. carbon content is between 92% and 98%.

questions.question: Which among the following constitute nucleus of an atom?
questions.explanation: The nucleus, that dense central core of the atom, contains both protons and neutrons. Electrons are outside the nucleus in energy levels.

questions.question: Which is referred to as Gobar gas?
questions.explanation: Bio gas is produced from cattle dung in a bio gas plant commonly known as gobar gas plant through a process called digestion.

questions.question: Which among the following radioactive element is used in food irradiation?
questions.explanation: Gamma rays are emitted from radioactive forms of the element cobalt (Cobalt 60) or of the element cesium (Cesium 137), which is used for food irradiation. Food irradiation (the application of ionizing radiation to food) is a technology that improves the safety and extends the shelf life of foods by reducing or eliminating microorganisms and insects.

questions.question: A cell will swell up in which type of solution?
questions.explanation: If the medium surrounding the cell has a higher water concentration than the cell, meaning that the outside solution is very dilute, the cell will gain water by osmosis. Such a solution is known as a hypotonic solution. As a result, the water enters the cell and swells up.

questions.question: Which among the following organelle performs the functions of storage, modification and packaging of products?
questions.explanation: The material synthesised near the ER is packaged and dispatched to various targets inside and outside the cell through the Golgi apparatus. Golgi Apparatus functions include the storage, modification and packaging of products in vesicles.

questions.question: Which among the following organelles is the powerhouse of the cell?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are known as the powerhouses of the cell. The energy required for various chemical activities needed for life is released by mitochondria in the form of ATP (Adenosine triphopshate) molecules.

questions.question: Which among the following part of brain helps in maintaining the posture and balance of the body?
questions.explanation: Cerebellum is responsible for precision of voluntary actions and maintaining the posture and balance of the body.

questions.question: Which component of blood transports nitrogenous waste?
questions.explanation: Blood consists of a fluid medium called plasma in which the cells are suspended. Plasma transports food, carbon dioxide and nitrogenous wastes in dissolved form.

questions.question: Cells were first discovered by __________?
questions.explanation: Cells were first discovered by Robert Hooke in 1665. He observed the cells in a cork slice with the help of a primitive microscope.

questions.question: Which among the following substances prevent desert plants from losing excess water?
questions.explanation: In desert plants, epidermis has a thick waxy coating of cutin (chemical substance with waterproof quality) on its outer surface. It is a water-impervious protective layer covering the epidermal cells of leaves and other parts and limiting water loss.

questions.question: Human hair is made up of _________?
questions.explanation: Hair is made of a tough protein called keratin. Keratin is extremely insoluble in water and organic solvents. It is the key structural material making up the outer layer of human skin.

questions.question: Concave mirrors are NOT used in which among the following situations?
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors are used in rear-view mirrors. Concave mirrors are commonly used in torches, search-lights and vehicles headlights to get powerful parallel beams of light. They are often used as shaving mirrors to see a larger image of the face. The dentists use concave mirrors to see large images of the teeth of patients. Large concave mirrors are used to concentrate sunlight to produce heat in solar furnaces.

questions.question: Which is referred to as converging lens?
questions.explanation: convex lenses are called converging lenses. Convex lens converges light rays.

questions.question: Power of a lens is the degree of _______?
questions.explanation: The degree of convergence or divergence of light rays achieved by a lens is expressed in terms of its power. The power of a lens is defined as the reciprocal of its focal length.

questions.question: Which human organ can produce magnetic field?
questions.explanation: Two main organs in the human body where the magnetic field produced is significant, are the heart and the brain. The magnetic field inside the body forms the basis of obtaining the images of different body parts. This is done using a technique called Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI).

questions.question: Which among the following process inside a fuse helps in serving its purpose?
questions.explanation: The use of an electric fuse prevents the electric circuit and the appliance from a possible damage by stopping the flow of unduly high electric current. The Joule heating that takes place in the fuse melts it to break the electric circuit.

questions.question: Electric generator works on the principle of _________?
questions.explanation: A generator converts mechanical energy into electrical energy. It works on the basis of electromagnetic induction.

questions.question: What is the temperature difference needed to generate current by Ocean-thermal energy conversion plants?
questions.explanation: The water at the surface of the sea or ocean is heated by the Sun while the water in deeper sections is relatively cold. This difference in temperature is exploited to obtain energy in ocean-thermal-energy conversion plants. These plants can operate if the temperature difference between the water at the surface and water at depths up to 2 km is 293 K (20°C) or more.

questions.question: Which part of eye converts light into electrical impulse?
questions.explanation: The retina is a delicate membrane having enormous number of light-sensitive cells. The light-sensitive cells get activated upon illumination and generate electrical signals. These signals are sent to the brain via the optic nerves.

questions.question: Phenomenon of splitting of light into its component colors is called as ______?
questions.explanation: The splitting of light into its component colours is called dispersion. The band of the coloured components of a light beam is called its spectrum.

questions.question: Which among the following phenomenon takes place in a rainbow?
questions.explanation: Rainbows are formed by sunlight entering a water droplet. They refract and disperse the incident sunlight, then reflect it internally, and finally refract it again when it comes out of the raindrop.

questions.question: When light undergoes tyndall effect, the color of the scattered light depends on ________?
questions.explanation: The colour of the scattered light depends on the size of the scattering particles. Very fine particles scatter mainly blue light while particles of larger size scatter light of longer wavelengths. If the size of the scattering particles is large enough, then, the scattered light may even appear white.

questions.question: A wooden box of "A" mass in moon, will require inertia __________?
options: Object doesn't require inertia in moon
Depends on the moon's gravity on object
questions.explanation: Greater the mass, the greater is the inertia. It remains the same whether the object is on the earth, the moon or even in outer space. Thus, the mass of an object is constant and does not change from place to place.
